texts.orig: Understanding the Potential of CCS in Hydrogen Production
texts.text: Understanding the Potential of CCS in Hydrogen Production

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · This presentation is the preliminary results of the IEAGHG funded Techno-Economic Study on H2 Production with CO2 Capture
texts.text: This presentation is the preliminary results of the IEAGHG funded Techno-Economic Study on H2 Production with CO2 Capture

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Like to acknowledge the work and contribution by AmecFW
texts.text: Like to acknowledge the work and contribution by AmecFW

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Overview to the production of hydrogen
texts.text: Overview to the production of hydrogen

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · H2 / HyCO from Fossil Fuel Technology Option
texts.text: H2 / HyCO from Fossil Fuel Technology Option

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Review of On-going Pilot & Demo Projects
texts.text: Review of On-going Pilot & Demo Projects

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Hydrogen is a key raw materials for various industries.
texts.text: Hydrogen is a key raw materials for various industries.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Hydrogen used could be in the form of high purity hydrogen (i.e. with greater than 99+% purity), or a mixture of hydrogen and carbon monoxide also known as HyCO gas.
texts.text: Hydrogen used could be in the form of high purity hydrogen (i.e. with greater than 99+% purity), or a mixture of hydrogen and carbon monoxide also known as HyCO gas.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Nearly 90% of global H2 or HyCO consumption
texts.text: Nearly 90% of global H2 or HyCO consumption

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Other large users of H2 or HyCO
texts.text: Other large users of H2 or HyCO

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · metal (i.e. DRI production), glass, food, electronics, space/aeronautic, fuel production (i.e. GTL), and other chemical & petrochemical industries (i.e. H2O2 production, paint)
texts.text: metal (i.e. DRI production), glass, food, electronics, space/aeronautic, fuel production (i.e. GTL), and other chemical & petrochemical industries (i.e. H2O2 production, paint)

texts.label: section_header
texts.orig: World's Hydrogen / HyCO Production (Key Facts) Data & figures from Air Products
texts.text: World's Hydrogen / HyCO Production (Key Facts) Data & figures from Air Products

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Around 95% of the H2 or HyCO produced is obtained from fossil fuels (natural gas, refinery off-gases, heavy refinery residues, petcoke, coal, and others).
texts.text: Around 95% of the H2 or HyCO produced is obtained from fossil fuels (natural gas, refinery off-gases, heavy refinery residues, petcoke, coal, and others).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Other sources could include water (via electrolysis), renewables, as well as byproduct from other chemical processes.
texts.text: Other sources could include water (via electrolysis), renewables, as well as byproduct from other chemical processes.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Conversion of fossil fuels to hydrogen also produces significant amount of CO2 as byproduct.
texts.text: Conversion of fossil fuels to hydrogen also produces significant amount of CO2 as byproduct.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Most of the modern hydrogen production facilities have already achieved efficiency that could reduce CO 2 emissions down to nearly 10% above its theoretical minimum.
texts.text: Most of the modern hydrogen production facilities have already achieved efficiency that could reduce CO 2 emissions down to nearly 10% above its theoretical minimum.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · SMR is the most dominant H2 production from fossil fuel (NG, light HC, Naptha).
texts.text: SMR is the most dominant H2 production from fossil fuel (NG, light HC, Naptha).

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Tuapse Refinery (Russia) - largest single train SMR @ ~240k Nm 3 /h  H2 supplied by Technip
texts.text: Tuapse Refinery (Russia) - largest single train SMR @ ~240k Nm 3 /h  H2 supplied by Technip

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · ATR is considered economical for large Syngas Production
texts.text: ATR is considered economical for large Syngas Production

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Oryx GTL (Qatar) - largest single train ATR @ ~ 577k Nm 3 /h syngas for FT Process supplied by Haldor Topsoe
texts.text: Oryx GTL (Qatar) - largest single train ATR @ ~ 577k Nm 3 /h syngas for FT Process supplied by Haldor Topsoe

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · POX are predominantly solid HC (coal, petcoke and other refinery residues) base H2 / Power / Syngas production. Gas based POX are limited in numbers worldwide.
texts.text: POX are predominantly solid HC (coal, petcoke and other refinery residues) base H2 / Power / Syngas production. Gas based POX are limited in numbers worldwide.

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Linde's La Porte POX Facility (USA) - largest single train gas based POX @ ~200k Nm 3 /h HyCO for chemical (methanol & others) production based on Shell Gasifier
texts.text: Linde's La Porte POX Facility (USA) - largest single train gas based POX @ ~200k Nm 3 /h HyCO for chemical (methanol & others) production based on Shell Gasifier

texts.label: text
texts.orig: By firing H2 fuel instead of NG/light HC, CO2 Capture of ~90% could be achievable for options #1 and #2.
texts.text: By firing H2 fuel instead of NG/light HC, CO2 Capture of ~90% could be achievable for options #1 and #2.

texts.text: Review of Large Scale / Demo CCS Projects for Gas Based H2 Production

texts.text: H2 Plant - SMR merchant plant owned and operated by Air Products

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · OTF supplier providing H2, steam & electricity to Valero Refinery
texts.text: OTF supplier providing H2, steam & electricity to Valero Refinery

texts.orig: Process Flow Diagram of Port Arthur II SMR Plant
texts.text: Process Flow Diagram of Port Arthur II SMR Plant

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Granted $284 MM  (66% of Total Cost - ~$430MM) under the Industrial CCS Demo Programme
texts.text: Granted $284 MM  (66% of Total Cost - ~$430MM) under the Industrial CCS Demo Programme

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · Construction started in Aug. 2011 (site prep. et. al.)
texts.text: Construction started in Aug. 2011 (site prep. et. al.)

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · CO2 Capture from Port Arthur II online in Dec. 2012 and Port Arthur I online in Mar. 2013. (Full capacity achieved in Apr. 2013)
texts.text: CO2 Capture from Port Arthur II online in Dec. 2012 and Port Arthur I online in Mar. 2013. (Full capacity achieved in Apr. 2013)

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · ~1.0 million tpy CO2 is captured  (50 MMscfd)
texts.text: ~1.0 million tpy CO2 is captured  (50 MMscfd)

texts.label: list_item
texts.orig: · CO2 recovery is >90% and CO2 purity is >97%
texts.text: CO2 recovery is >90% and CO2 purity is >97%
